channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Phase-controlled molecular beam deposition unlocks flexible MgAgSb thermoelectrics with exceptional performance
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 12 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-69451-8

This study presents a high-performance flexible thermoelectric film and device based on eco-friendly MgAgSb. Precise phase and composition control enable high power factor and output performance, highlighting its potential for flexible electronics.
item.dc:title: Phase-controlled molecular beam deposition unlocks flexible MgAgSb thermoelectrics with exceptional performance
item.dc:creator: Zhao Hu
Airan Li
Naoki Sato
Xinzhi Wu
Longquan Wang
Xinyuan Wang
Takashi Aizawa
Takao Mori

item.title: Interfacial stress decoupling enables stable palladium-based hydrogen sensing
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 12 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-69499-6

This study presents an interfacial stress decoupling strategy using dithiol-based self-assembled layers enabling stable palladium hydrogen sensing, demonstrating wafer-scale device fabrication and seamless integration of a portable detection platform.
item.dc:title: Interfacial stress decoupling enables stable palladium-based hydrogen sensing
item.dc:creator: Rui Gao
Guozhu Zhang
Xiaoyuan Wang
Yujing Xu
Chao Zhang
Linfeng Li
Zeyu Wang
Bowei Zhang
Kazuki Nagashima
Takeshi Yanagida
Fu-Zhen Xuan
